description:
You are a container-platform specialist performing a quick security check of the in-repo Dockerfiles used by three micro-services.

    PRE-EXISTING SET-UP
    • The project root is /home/user/microservices  
      ├── service-a/Dockerfile  
      ├── service-b/Dockerfile  
      └── service-c/Dockerfile  

    WHAT TO DO
    1. Perform a case-insensitive recursive search that locates every line in every Dockerfile that pulls an image tagged exactly “latest”.  
       • A single terminal command is enough (pipe or redirection is fine).  
       • Search scope is restricted to /home/user/microservices.

    2. Write the result to the file /home/user/scan/latest_tag_findings.log.  
       • The directory /home/user/scan already exists and is writable.  
       • Use truncation (overwrite if the file happens to be present).

    REQUIRED LOG FORMAT (strictly, the automated checker will diff this):
       Each finding must be on its own line with the format  
           <relative-path-from-/home/user/microservices>:<line-number>:<entire-matching-line>  
       Examples (DO NOT hard-code):  
           service-x/Dockerfile:3:FROM ubuntu:latest  
           service-y/Dockerfile:1:FROM nginx:latest

       Notes:  
       • “relative-path-from-/home/user/microservices” means do not include the leading “/home/user/microservices/”.  
       • Use UNIX line endings.  
       • Preserve all spacing in the matched line exactly as it appears in the Dockerfile.

    VERIFICATION HINTS
    • Exactly one line is expected in the final log for the current repo state.  
    • The command should finish without interactive prompts.

    Go ahead and generate /home/user/scan/latest_tag_findings.log in the specified format.
truth:
Initial file tree before the agent starts:

    /home/user/microservices/service-a/Dockerfile
        1  FROM python:3.9-slim
        2  # Simple API service

    /home/user/microservices/service-b/Dockerfile
        1  FROM ubuntu:latest
        2  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl

    /home/user/microservices/service-c/Dockerfile
        1  FROM node:14
        2  WORKDIR /usr/src/app

    The directory /home/user/scan exists and is empty.

    Expected file AFTER the agent succeeds:

    /home/user/scan/latest_tag_findings.log
        service-b/Dockerfile:1:FROM ubuntu:latest\n  (exact trailing newline)

    No other files or directories should be modified or created.
